reviews.review_msg: Brenda was approachable and made it comfortable to ask questions about the topics. She gave me detailed comments on the drafts for each of our two big assignments. The only downside was it sometimes took a while to get them back but they helped me improve my writing. She also gave great suggestions in office hours. Overall a good course.

took Brenda for IS350. Brenda made us submit all the assignments like 3 or 4 separate times and provided little to no feedback despite this course being designed for improving writing. Overall Brenda was quite confusing and her lectures were repetitive.

Professor Lyshaug used slides for every lecture and went through them at a fair pace; readings online are easily accessible before and after lectures. She regularly holds office hours and often replies relatively quickly to emailed questions. Throughout the semester, she was helpful in providing clear criteria for the midterm and final.

I really enjoyed this course and learned a lot. The professor explained concepts clearly and was good at connecting ideas to real examples which helped everything make more sense. She was very approachable and encouraged questions. The exams were essays but she gave the topics out ahead of time so it wasn't too hard.

She's insanely unorganized and has terrible time management. Class would always run overtime because she couldn't fit all the content in, as she would ramble on. You didn't know what your grade was throughout the course either. The amount of weekly readings given for an intro course was insane, and exams are heavily based on the readings.
Lectures were pre-recorded and tutorials were in person, class was pretty easy and really interesting

terrible time management, always goes over time without covering everything and without giving a break. assigns sometimes hundreds of pages of readings a week, which are almost necessary because her lectures are full of her rambling. unclear grading with no formal rubrics.

seems like a super nice lady, but not the best instructor.
i did okay because i was interested in the topic, had some background knowledge and had fun reading, but it was hard for an introductory course

Brenda seems like the nicest person but IS302 was the most boring class I have taken at SFU. She assigns 100-120 pages of readings each week. There are weekly discussions and a paper and final exam. Seems like a nice person, but the amount of readings required for one class is insane.
